title: chambeyronia oliviformis
intro: Chambeyronia oliviformis, also known as the "red bumpy boxwood," is a species of plant that belongs to the family Sapindaceae. Traditionally, Chambeyronia oliviformis has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. The health benefits associated with this plant include its ability to reduce inflammation, alleviate pain, and support the immune system. Chambeyronia oliviformis is often used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and skin conditions.
